title: STAT+: Abivax ulcerative colitis drug shows strong efficacy, but cases of cancer raise concerns
summary: Abivax said its experimental treatment for ulcerative colitis showed significant efficacy in a closely watched trial, but shares of the company tanked on safety concerns.

title: STAT+: At ASCO, talk of barriers to cancer care, new treatments, and other big takeaways
summary: Here is some of what you may have missed from the world's largest cancer research meeting.

title: STAT+: At the world’s largest cancer research meeting, data briefly took a backseat to grief
summary: At the world's largest cancer research meeting, new data were plentiful. But this year, there was also a focus on grief.

title: RallyBio tries again for reverse merger, this time with Avenzo
summary: It's the second attempt for the rare disease company, which initially announced plans to merge with Candid Therapeutics before the immune drug developer was bought by UCB.

title: Servier buys into muscular dystrophy with potentially $2.7B deal
summary: The French drugmaker has agreed to acquire an experimental tablet from Edgewise Therapeutics that some bullish analysts say could eventually hit peak sales of over $2 billion.

title: As FDA misses deadline on electric shock ban, disability advocates speak out
summary: The FDA has missed a self-imposed deadline to ban the use of electrical shock devices in people with intellectual disabilities.

title: STAT+: Medicare’s mum on GLP-1s, and Massachusetts sues United
summary: We've been asking Medicare for weeks about the cost of giving seniors $50 obesity drugs.

title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about pancreatic and lung cancer drugs, China biotech growth, and more
summary: Detailed study data for a pancreatic cancer treatment from Revolution Medicines confirmed it increased overall survival, from 13.2 months to 6.7 months

title: Opinion: What should hospitals do when patients demand unvaccinated donor blood?
summary: “You have to understand a person's belief if you want to address it,” Deva Sharma says on the “First Opinion Podcast” about requests for unvaccinated donor blood.

title: STAT+: A turning point for pancreatic cancer patients
summary: Eli Lilly announces two China deals, Akeso's lung cancer bispecific payoff, and more biotech news from The Readout

title: CDC says containing the spread of Ebola is top priority
summary: The Trump administration disregards infectious disease playbook, CEPI offers vaccine funding, and other health news from Morning Rounds

title: STAT+: Servier to buy Edgewise Therapeutics’ muscular dystrophy drug
summary: The French pharma firm Servier is buying an experimental muscular dystrophy drug from Edgewise Therapeutics for $1.55 billion upfront.

title: 2026 Orphan Drugs Report: A safe(r) passage amid stormy waters?
summary: Orphan drugs remain commercially durable, strategically relevant and supported by a strong pipeline

title: STAT+: No Surprises Act arbitration rule could worsen flood of provider disputes
summary: The arbitration process has protected patients but generated financial windfalls for some clinicians.

title: The hidden history of BPC-157, a favorite MAHA peptide
summary: A compound named “substance God help me” by its longtime champion could benefit from RFK Jr.'s favorable views on using peptides.

title: STAT+: Medicare still won’t say how much covering obesity drugs will cost
summary: Older adults on Medicare can get Wegovy and Zepbound for $50 a month, starting in July. But the agency still is not sharing how much this will cost taxpayers.

title: STAT+: Executives reveal tech initiatives at Summa Health, eight months after General Catalyst acquisition
summary: Two years after General Catalyst said it was buying Ohio safety-net hospital Summa Health, executives shed light on how the “transformation” is going.

title: Opinion: Medical schools diversified. So where are all the diverse doctors?
summary: Why are residency programs disciplining and dismissing trainees in a system funded to train them?

title: Opinion: The military’s medical corps has a serious recruitment problem
summary: Congress must incentivize more physicians (and their employers) to join the military medical corps, writes the former attending physician to Congress.

title: STAT+: A standing ovation for RevMed, and caution for Akeso
summary: ASCO Day 3: A huge advance for pancreatic cancer, a let down for Akeso and Summit, and a new approach to immunotherapy.

title: Ultra-low doses could bring costly cancer treatments to more patients in poorer countries
summary: Low-dose, low-cost immunotherapies may help patients in poorer countries access high-tech cancer treatments.

title: STAT+: For prostate cancer patients set on surgery, new hormone regimen may improve outcomes, study finds
summary: By doing hormone therapy upfront in prostate cancer cases, the hope was to shrink the relapse rate after surgery. Results were encouraging.

title: STAT+: Akeso and Summit’s ivonescimab extends survival in squamous cell lung cancer
summary: A lung cancer drug developed in China was a highlight of ASCO but doctors want to see follow-up in a more diverse population.

title: ‘Unprecedented’ Revolution data point to paradigm shift in pancreatic cancer
summary: Highly anticipated data presented at ASCO confirmed what’s been heralded as a medical breakthrough against a historically tough-to-treat tumor.

title: Akeso, Summit drug extends survival in closely watched lung cancer trial
summary: Though ivonescimab reduced the risk of death by one-third compared to chemotherapy in a China-based study, the total survival benefit of four months stirred debate and pressured company shares.

title: At ASCO, Merck makes case for a ‘cornerstone’ cancer drug
summary: Study results accrued so far are giving Merck greater conviction that the therapy, licensed from a China-based biotech, was worth placing into a sprawling Phase 3 program.

title: Bristol Myers unveils data for one of its next-gen blood cancer drugs
summary: Called mezigdomide, the drug is one of two protein-degrading medicines Bristol Myers hopes to soon position as successors to its lucrative Revlimid franchise.

title: Replimune takes third swing at cancer drug approval following ‘productive’ FDA talks
summary: A twice-spurned therapy that’s been seen as one of the FDA’s more controversial recent rejections is getting a new chance following the resignation of former commissioner Marty Makary.

title: Allogene CEO to step down; Agios drug fails key test
summary: David Chang will leave his post after an eight-year run at the cell therapy maker. Elsewhere, a next-generation blood disease drug stumbled and an acquisition yielded an FDA approval for AbbVie.

title: Can a chaotic FDA still deliver on faster drug development?
summary: As leadership changes create more instability at the FDA, here’s what one former agency official thinks it will take to keep speedier drug approvals on track.

title: FDA Issues Draft Guidance to Cut Unnecessary Animal Testing for Cancer Drugs
summary: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today issued a draft guidance to reduce unnecessary animal testing in nonclinical safety assessments for certain cancer drugs.
